title: Diploma in Mechanical Engineering (Electric Vehicle)
content: The Program is created to give participants a thorough introduction to the skill set needed for the EV revolution. Electric vehicle technology, power electronics for electric vehicles, and testing and certification of electric and hybrid vehicles are all included in this skill set. It contains the foundational and useful features of the pertinent gadgets and technology. 10th with minimum 40% marks. For Lateral Entry: Or 10th (minimum 40%) + 2 year ITI (minimum 40%) shall be eligible for admission to Second Year Diploma Course(s) in Any branch of Engineering and Technology. 01 To produce highly skilled engineers who can contribute to the advancement of sustainable transportation technologies. 02 The program aims to equip students with the fundamental knowledge of mechanical engineering principles and specialize their expertise in the design, development, and analysis of electric vehicles and their components. 03 This includes a strong emphasis on electric vehicle powertrains, battery technology, energy management systems, and the integration of various engineering disciplines for optimal vehicle performance and efficiency. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice 08 09 Communication: An ability to communicate effectively. 10 01 Graduates shall apply fundamental and advanced knowledge of electrical engineering and electric vehicle technology to address industry challenges and promote e-mobility solutions. 02 Graduates shall demonstrate research aptitude and problem-solving abilities in a multidisciplinary environment and pursue higher education or certifications in emerging areas of EV technology. 03 Graduates shall develop professional ethics and an entrepreneurial mindset, enabling them to contribute to the electric vehicle industry and support sustainable societal development. 01 Students will be proficient in designing, analyzing, and integrating key components of electric vehicle systems, including battery management systems, power electronics, electric drives, and control systems. 02 Students will be capable of designing and implementing sustainable electric vehicle solutions, including the development of charging infrastructure and energy management strategies. 10th with minimum 40% marks. For Lateral Entry: Or 10th (minimum 40%) + 2 year ITI (minimum 40%) shall be eligible for admission to Second Year Diploma Course(s) in Any branch of Engineering and Technology.
